# RFC-0003: Server, Recipe, And Runtime Execution Contract

> **Version:** 0.8.0 | **Status:** normative | **Phase:** test

---

## 1. Summary

### [RFC-0003:C-SUMMARY] Summary (Informative) <a id="rfc-0003c-summary"></a>

This RFC governs independently launchable servers, server-owned cases, recipes, and runtime execution: typed resolution before external effects, long-running and closed-loop lifecycles including image-backed launches, canonical single and prefill/decode role topologies, per-role parallelism, and routing ownership. [RFC-0001](../rfc/RFC-0001.md) holds the specification overview and authority map.

*Since: v0.1.0*

---

## 2. Specification

### [RFC-0003:C-RECIPE-COMPOSITION] Server, Case, And Recipe Composition (Normative) <a id="rfc-0003c-recipe-composition"></a>

A server MUST be an independently launchable workspace definition. Its `stack`, `model`, and `topology` fields MUST select exactly one stack, model, and supported topology; its common behavior and canonical `roles` supply the typed base inherited by its cases.

A single topology MUST use the canonical role `serve`. A prefill/decode topology MUST use canonical roles `prefill` and `decode`; its routing resolution derives the canonical role `router`. Workspace configuration MUST reject `roles.router` and every other declared `router` role. It MUST NOT permit alternate names for canonical roles or a public role `kind` field; topology and role key determine role identity.

A server MAY define identifier-keyed `cases` and an optional `default_case`. Each case MUST be a typed behavior patch over that server. A case MUST NOT change the selected stack, model, topology, canonical role identities, case set, placement, model locator, or canonical router role and cardinality invariants. An empty case is valid.

A declared `default_case` MUST reference one owned case. A server with no cases MUST resolve its base behavior when case selection is omitted and MUST reject `--case`. A server with exactly one case MUST select that case when case selection is omitted. A server with more than one case MUST declare a valid `default_case`; omission MUST select it. Explicit case selection MUST name one case owned by the selected server.

A recipe MUST select exactly one `server` and one `workload_suite`. It MUST NOT own, copy, filter, reorder, or redefine the server's cases.

A workload suite MUST name an ordered sequence of Eval and Bench measurements and MAY name one Eval as its gate. A gated Bench is skipped when the gate does not succeed ([RFC-0004:C-MEASUREMENTS](../rfc/RFC-0004.md#rfc-0004c-measurements)).

Every referenced model, stack, server, measurement, workload suite, and selected case MUST resolve within the selected workspace revision before the first external effect.

An ordinary operator MUST be able to select a server directly or select a recipe and, when applicable, one server case without manually composing referenced definitions.

A recipe MUST define bounded server-plus-measurement composition. The public contract MUST NOT require users to author arbitrary task nodes or a general workflow DAG.

**Rationale:** Server intent has an independent lifecycle and reuse boundary: the same server is started directly, consumed by multiple recipes, attached to a manual Bench, and referenced by image validation. Server-owned typed cases vary behavior without giving recipes a second authority. Fixed role names let placement, records, and integrations share one vocabulary without turning topology into a general graph language.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0003:C-RESOLUTION] Resolution Dry Run And Overrides (Normative) <a id="rfc-0003c-resolution"></a>

Server resolution MUST use one ordered path: load the workspace; select the server, case, stack, and model; load the permitted local bindings and select placement; merge typed behavior patches; validate realization compatibility; invoke integration planning; allocate ranks, machine-specific model locators, ports, caches, and network facts; invoke final integration rendering; then freeze one immutable resolved execution object.

Behavior precedence MUST be server base, then the selected server case, then invocation-scoped overrides in command-line order. Resolution MUST preserve the ordered common and role declaration layers and the source of every effective behavior-affecting value.

An invocation-scoped override MUST use repeatable `--set <TOML-KEY-PATH>=<TOML-VALUE>`. TOML quoted key segments MUST allow a literal dot in a key. Values MUST be parsed as TOML values. Repeated overrides apply left to right, and the last assignment to the same path wins.

A `serve start` override path MUST be rooted at the selected server through `server`. A `recipe run` override path MAY be rooted at `server`, `evals.<eval-id>`, or `benches.<bench-id>` under [RFC-0004:C-RECIPE-MEASUREMENT-OVERRIDES](../rfc/RFC-0004.md#rfc-0004c-recipe-measurement-overrides). Framework setting paths MUST use `server.settings.<key>` for common values or `server.roles.<role>.settings.<key>` for role values. A core server field name MUST NOT be reinterpreted as a framework setting.

Cases and invocation overrides MUST use the same typed patch model. An override MAY change only typed server behavior fields and entries under an explicit framework `settings` map. It MUST NOT change server identity, stack, model, topology, canonical role identities, case definitions, case selection, placement, model locators, or canonical router role and cardinality invariants.

Workspace server configuration MUST be able to declare a framework-neutral readiness timeout independently of framework settings. An integration response MUST NOT replace it. A capture-armed launch waits without that bound under [RFC-0004:C-WORKLOAD-PROFILING](../rfc/RFC-0004.md#rfc-0004c-workload-profiling).

Server cases and invocation-scoped server overrides MUST be able to patch `readiness_timeout_seconds` through the typed server patch model.

The selected realization MUST be validated against the resolved server before any state-affecting command. A local realization MUST use the server stack's Pixi environment. A built image MUST realize the same stack. A declared external image MUST carry an immutable digest and claim an integration compatible with the server stack; it does not claim stack equality.

Local integration operations MUST execute in the selected stack's Pixi environment. Image-backed integration operations MUST execute against the selected image realization. Integration operations and external probes used during resolution MUST be read-only.

The frozen resolved object MUST be the sole authority for dry-run output, state-affecting execution, cleanup, and record production. Execution MUST NOT reload configuration, reselect defaults, rerun placement choice, or reconstruct server, case, adapter, endpoint, command, or measurement facts after that object is frozen.

Dry-run MUST perform the same reference resolution, case selection, typed patching, placement compatibility, determinable resource and toolchain validation, integration planning and rendering, rank allocation, locator resolution, endpoint allocation, cache allocation, and command construction required before real execution. It MUST NOT launch a server, execute a measurement, acquire persistent server-operation ownership, execute realization checks, or create a record that claims execution occurred.

Manual Bench resolution MUST combine one named Bench definition from the discovered workspace root, one explicit running server record stored under that root, the immutable endpoint, model, and server-control capabilities in that record, the Bench toolchain owned by the invoking Inferlab release, and invocation-scoped Bench overrides.

A manual Bench MAY use a newer or dirty measurement workspace snapshot than the serving snapshot. It MUST reject a target record whose record schema or preserved server capabilities are incompatible with the invoking release.

A manual Bench override MUST use repeatable `--set <path=value>`, patch the selected typed Bench definition in command-line order, and be fully validated before case expansion. It MUST NOT change Bench identity or kind, target server record, endpoint, model, serving integration, or local bindings.

A Bench that requests prefix-cache reset MUST fail preflight when the target server record does not preserve a compatible reset capability.

For a static Bench matrix, dry-run MUST report every exact expanded case in execution order. For an adaptive Bench, it MUST report the initial probes, target metric and threshold, refinement limit, configured minimum resolution, and adaptive policy identity. Each adaptive next case MUST be resolved from recorded prior observations according to that reported policy before it starts.

**Rationale:** One resolution path prevents dry-run, execution, and evidence from drifting. TOML-path overrides retain rapid experiment iteration without creating persistent local behavior or an untyped shell surface. Freezing after both integration phases means every later external action consumes an already validated, fully concrete execution rather than rediscovering configuration under changing files or defaults.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0003:C-RUNTIME-WORKFLOWS] Runtime Workflows (Normative) <a id="rfc-0003c-runtime-workflows"></a>

Inferlab MUST support a long-running server workflow in which an operator starts one resolved server behavior, observes its status and logs, and explicitly stops it.

Inferlab MUST support a closed-loop recipe workflow in which Inferlab resolves the recipe's server and effective case selection, starts it, waits for readiness, executes the workload suite, and stops the server.

Both workflows MUST use the same server and case resolution, validation, integration planning and rendering, launch, readiness, runtime-handle, and cleanup behavior.

A successfully started long-running server record MUST remain running until an explicit stop or recovery action finalizes it. Its resolved recipe identity MUST be null. A recipe record MUST preserve its recipe identity and selected server and case.

Inferlab is an experiment harness, not a production supervisor. It MUST NOT automatically restart experiment servers or claim availability guarantees.

During a live start or closed-loop command, Inferlab MUST attempt cleanup after handled launch, readiness, measurement, interruption, or cancellation failure. A closed-loop recipe run MUST also attempt cleanup after success. Recovery after the Inferlab process or host terminates before a runtime handle is durable MAY require operator cleanup, and the record MUST NOT claim verified cleanup.

The public runtime CLI MUST provide:

- `serve start <server>`, accepting optional `--case`, `--placement`, mutually exclusive `--image` and `--external-image`, repeatable `--set`, `--local`, and `--dry-run`;
- `serve status <record>`, `serve logs <record>`, and `serve stop <record>`;
- `recipe run <recipe>`, accepting the same server realization selectors plus `--capture`;
- `bench <bench> --serve <record>`, accepting repeatable `--set`, `--capture`, and `--dry-run`.

`serve start` MUST select the named server directly. `recipe run` MUST select the server named by the recipe. Both MUST resolve exactly one effective server behavior and zero or one case identity under [RFC-0003:C-RECIPE-COMPOSITION](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-recipe-composition).

`--local` MUST be accepted only by `serve start`, `recipe run`, and `image build` among these governed runtime workflows. Server `status`, `logs`, and `stop` MUST resolve lifecycle facts solely from the named record and MUST NOT load shared workspace configuration or local bindings.

After the resolved object is frozen and a durable record exists, real launch MUST execute stack realization checks and machine hardware probes, materialize launch files, then spawn model-serving ranks in canonical order: `serve`, or `prefill` then `decode`, with replica and rank indices ascending. A router MUST spawn last. Readiness evaluation MUST begin only after every resolved process has spawned. Rollback and cleanup MUST proceed in reverse spawn order.

A routed topology MUST NOT be reported ready until every required model-serving backend and its public router endpoint are ready. A handled partial-start failure MUST attempt rollback of every process already started.

`serve start` and `recipe run` MAY select a successful image build record. The selected host-platform assembly substitutes for the local stack realization through the same containerized substitution used by image validation ([RFC-0007:C-IMAGE-BUILD](../rfc/RFC-0007.md#rfc-0007c-image-build)); all other server resolution, measurement, evidence, and cleanup semantics remain unchanged.

Image-backed resolution MUST reject profiling capture until this specification defines an in-container profiler contract, because a host profiler wrapping the container client does not observe the server process.

Built-image integration planning and rendering MUST execute against the selected image realization through its entrypoint and one-shot JSON contract, consuming the integration packages baked into the image. It MUST NOT require a locally installed stack environment. A failed or timed-out adapter invocation MUST NOT leave its container running.

Local bindings MAY declare an image-adapter deadline and, only for a host whose container runtime rejects device-less container creation, one workaround device. The adapter container MUST request no device when none is declared and no capability beyond that workaround.

A built-image selection MUST be rejected before any state-affecting command or integration invocation when the record's image stack differs from the server stack, when it has no successful assembly for the launching host platform, or when the selected placement is not entirely local to the machine whose builder storage holds the image. Inferlab defines no distribution of a built image between machines.

A built-image server record MUST preserve the immutable image identity, image build record, image workspace revision, and invoking workspace revision so qualification drift remains observable.

A containerized launch MUST receive every resolver- and integration-set process environment variable explicitly and MUST NOT infer that set by comparison with the ambient environment.

Each `external_images` definition MUST contain only a digest-pinned `reference` and an `integration` claim; it MUST NOT claim a stack. A declaration without an immutable digest, with an invalid integration identifier, with any additional field, or with a colliding identifier MUST be rejected at workspace load.

An external image and built-image selection MUST be mutually exclusive. External-image resolution MUST validate the claimed integration against the server stack's integration, not claim stack equality. It MUST verify by read-only probe that the digest exists in builder storage on the controller and every launch machine, MUST NOT pull it, and MUST fail a missing image naming the machine and exact pull command.

An external-image placement MAY span machines. A remote containerized launch MUST NOT require a remote workspace realization: the image replaces the serving stack environment and the remote workspace path is only a launch directory. Machine-scoped user, launch, environment pass-through, device, capability, and cache facts MUST resolve on each launch machine and enter the record.

Each declared environment pass-through value MUST be resolved on its launch machine; an absent value MUST be reported naming that machine. On a remote launch, the value MUST expand on that machine and MUST NOT enter the resolved plan, recorded command, or launch-script text. A local launch records its effective environment under the standing unredacted-record posture.

The containerized substitution MUST lower each launch machine's declared container hardware facts ([RFC-0007:C-IMAGE-BUILD](../rfc/RFC-0007.md#rfc-0007c-image-build)) into every server container: host devices, unlimited pinned-memory ulimit, and capabilities. It MUST NOT request privileged mode.

Containerized processes launched locally or over SSH MUST carry a resolver-assigned container name as a runtime handle. Cleanup verification MUST confirm removal on the launch machine; an already absent container is confirmation. A launch failure after a container may exist MUST attempt removal using that durable name. Killing only the container client MUST NOT be reported as verified server cleanup.

A built image MUST run through its activation entrypoint. An external image MUST run through an explicit command override and assumes nothing about the image's own entrypoint. External-image adapter lowering MUST use the workspace-pinned integration packages from the committed framework-free Pixi environment named `adapter`, made available to the adapter operation without treating that environment as a serving stack or requiring the serving framework locally. Resolution MUST observe and record the framework version in the external image and reject an image in which the claimed framework is not observable.

An external-image launch is not qualified by the workspace. Its record MUST preserve a distinct realization state, make no stack-check claim, and carry the declaration identifier, digest, integration claim, and observed framework version. Manual Bench admission MUST otherwise treat local, built-image, and external-image managed servers identically.

`serve start`, `recipe run`, and `bench` MUST support dry-run under [RFC-0003:C-RESOLUTION](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-resolution).

A manual `bench` MUST target one explicit managed server record, MUST NOT select an implicit latest server, and MUST reject a record not in running state or whose typed runtime handles do not identify live processes. It MUST NOT require a separate endpoint-readiness probe; endpoint request failure remains Bench failure evidence.

No more than one manual Bench may execute against one managed server at a time. Bench acceptance and `serve stop` acceptance for that server MUST be mutually exclusive. Every handled Bench terminal outcome MUST finalize its record, terminate owned measurement process groups after timeout, interruption, or cancellation, and release server-operation ownership.

A measurement client MUST have a durable process-group handle with leader start-time identity before its first experiment effect. A later run in the same workspace MUST terminate an identity-matching survivor of an unclean exit and MUST NOT signal a process group whose identity no longer matches.

Stale operation ownership MUST NOT permanently block a later Bench or stop. Recovery MUST verify that no live owner remains and preserve its outcome when an operation record exists.

This specification does not define separate serve-scoped Eval or Bench subcommands.

**Rationale:** The two server workflows differ only in who owns the server after readiness; direct server selection no longer requires a synthetic recipe. Record-only lifecycle commands remain operable from frozen evidence even if configuration changes. Deterministic spawn and reverse cleanup order make heterogeneous multi-process failures diagnosable. Built and external images reuse the same resolved server contract while keeping their distinct distribution and qualification claims honest.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0003:C-SERVE-TOPOLOGY] Serve Topology And Role Graph (Normative) <a id="rfc-0003c-serve-topology"></a>

Inferlab MUST support two public server topologies: `single` and `prefill_decode`. They are product workflows, not arbitrary role graphs.

A resolved topology MUST contain canonical logical roles, independently routable replicas within those roles, and process ranks that realize those replicas. Every rendered process MUST belong to exactly one role, one replica, and one rank.

A `single` topology MUST contain exactly one `serve` replica. It MUST expose that replica's rank-zero endpoint directly and MUST NOT declare routing-backend or KV-transfer selection.

A `prefill_decode` topology MUST contain independent positive replica counts for `prefill` and `decode` and exactly one derived `router` replica. Its public workload endpoint MUST be the router endpoint.

Each role's declared replica count MUST equal its resolved replica count. Replica identity MUST be unique within a role. Each replica MUST contain one or more ranks whose indices are unique and contiguous from zero.

Each model-serving replica MUST have exactly one rank-zero serving entry point. Nonzero ranks are headless participants and MUST NOT be treated as independently routable backends. Routing, KV transfer, bootstrap, and side-channel targeting MUST use rank-zero endpoints unless a backend-specific clause explicitly defines a non-routing internal dependency.

The canonical router MUST contain only replica zero and rank zero, have empty effective parallelism, and demand zero devices. Workspace server configuration MUST NOT declare it directly.

A single model-serving replica MAY span machines through explicit local rank placement. Different roles and replicas MAY have different device counts, rank counts, machine sets, ports, settings, and parallelism.

Role links MUST distinguish request routing, KV transfer, bootstrap, and side-channel semantics. A role link between prefill and decode sets MUST NOT imply one-to-one replica pairing; every actual rank-zero target and routing policy MUST be explicit in the resolved result.

The selected integration MUST plan canonical roles, replica counts, complete per-role effective behavior, whole-replica device count, directed links, port and endpoint requirements, readiness, profiler controls, server-control actions, and routing ownership. It MUST NOT choose concrete process-rank counts, machines, devices, ports, model locators, or runtime storage during planning.

The control plane MUST apply [RFC-0002:C-LOCAL-PLACEMENT](../rfc/RFC-0002.md#rfc-0002c-local-placement), allocate ranks, machines, devices, ports, model locators, cache roots, and network facts, then supply those concrete allocations to final integration rendering.

The selected integration MUST render exactly one process for every supplied integration-owned allocation and MUST preserve its process, role, replica, and rank identity. Inferlab-owned router allocations are not supplied to integration rendering.

The control plane MUST validate role identities, replica cardinality, rank continuity, process bindings, links, resource totals, launch dependencies, routing ownership, and endpoint ownership before the first launch.

A resolved topology MUST expose exactly one public workload endpoint. Single and prefill/decode topologies MUST use the same runtime-handle, rollback, readiness, status, logs, stop, recovery, and cleanup semantics.

The vLLM integration MUST support `prefill_decode` over Mooncake and NIXL with independent positive prefill and decode replica counts.

The required role, replica, rank, allocation, and routing shapes MUST use adapter protocol version 5 under [RFC-0006:C-INTEGRATIONS](../rfc/RFC-0006.md#rfc-0006c-integrations). An adapter advertising an earlier protocol MUST NOT emit or consume them.

**Rationale:** Roles describe serving responsibility, replicas describe independently routable instances, and ranks describe concrete processes. Keeping those identities distinct expresses heterogeneous nP/mD and cross-machine replicas without turning portable server configuration into machine topology or promoting a general deployment graph.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0003:C-SERVE-PARALLELISM] Component-Aware Serve Parallelism (Normative) <a id="rfc-0003c-serve-parallelism"></a>

A server, its canonical model-serving roles, its cases, and invocation-scoped overrides MAY declare framework-neutral component-aware parallelism patches.

The component-aware shape MUST expose these optional positive-integer components: `outer.tensor_parallel_size`, `outer.pipeline_parallel_size`, `attention.tensor_parallel_size`, `attention.data_parallel_size`, `attention.context_parallel_size`, `experts.tensor_parallel_size`, `experts.data_parallel_size`, `experts.expert_parallel_size`, and `experts.dense_tensor_parallel_size`.

A server-level parallelism shape is only an inheritance patch for model-serving roles. For each role, resolution MUST merge server common values, server role values, selected-case common and role values, and invocation-scoped values under [RFC-0003:C-RESOLUTION](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-resolution). The resulting per-role request is the declared role shape.

An operator MUST be able to override one component without repeating unaffected components.

For every model-serving role, the selected integration MUST either preserve every explicitly declared component or reject the combination as unsupported before launch. It MUST fill every omitted component needed to return one complete positive-integer effective shape for that role.

The sole effective parallelism authority MUST be each role's complete effective shape. The integration protocol and resolved execution MUST NOT return or reconstruct a server-wide effective parallelism shape.

The canonical router role MUST have an empty effective shape and zero device count.

Prefill and decode MAY resolve to different tensor, pipeline, data, context, expert, and dense tensor parallelism, different whole-replica device count, different replica counts, and different rank decompositions.

Dry-run output and records MUST preserve common and role declarations, the source of each declared component, and each role's complete effective shape.

Placement MUST consume whole-replica device count derived from each complete effective role shape.

The framework-neutral configuration and protocol MUST NOT expose framework-native flags or framework-specific parallelism algebra.

These required per-role shapes MUST use adapter protocol version 5. An adapter advertising an earlier version MUST NOT emit or consume them.

**Rationale:** Common values remove repetition but do not create a second effective authority. Per-role effective shapes are necessary because prefill and decode can use different card counts and decomposition, while the integration remains responsible for each framework's algebra and unsupported combinations.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0003:C-EXPERIMENTAL-PROXY] Routing Ownership And Public Endpoint (Normative) <a id="rfc-0003c-experimental-proxy"></a>

Integration planning MUST return one closed routing ownership result: `direct`, `inferlab_builtin`, or `integration_native`. It MUST NOT return an arbitrary public-endpoint requirement.

A `single` topology MUST resolve to `direct`, expose its sole `serve` replica's rank-zero endpoint, and have no router allocation.

A `prefill_decode` topology MUST expose one canonical `router` role as its public workload endpoint. Its request-routing backend and KV-transfer mechanism MUST remain independently selected, validated, and recorded.

An unsupported combination of integration, topology, routing backend, or KV-transfer mechanism MUST fail resolution before any process launches. A P/D server that omits a routing backend MUST select `builtin` only when the integration advertises a compatible Inferlab-owned implementation.

For `inferlab_builtin`, integration planning MUST return one closed implementation kind from `vllm_mooncake`, `vllm_nixl`, `sglang`, or `trtllm`, together with its routing policy, target semantics, ports, readiness, public endpoint contract, and server-control actions. Rust MUST derive the one canonical router allocation, launch the Inferlab-owned implementation, and MUST NOT supply that allocation to `render_serve`. The router allocation MUST be controller-local, replica zero, rank zero, and use no devices.

For `integration_native`, integration planning MUST return the canonical router role with exactly one zero-device replica, empty effective parallelism, framework-specific settings, `device_count` zero, ports, readiness, links, endpoint contract, and render inputs. Rust MUST place that router through the same local-placement authority as other processes and MUST supply its concrete allocation to `render_serve`. A native router MAY run remotely.

A framework-native router MUST be supplied by the selected stack realization or selected image and MUST NOT require a separate global tool catalog or environment authority.

An integration MUST NOT advertise a routing backend unless it can validate the settings, declare routing targets and policy, render a native invocation when it owns one, and declare readiness, endpoint, and control requirements.

Every router MUST target only rank-zero serving entry points. Role links MUST NOT imply replica pairing; the planned routing policy and complete target sets are authoritative.

Inferlab MUST allocate concrete internal endpoints and retain lifecycle authority for every router regardless of implementation owner. Router commands, environment, logs, readiness, durable handles, failures, rollback, and cleanup MUST have the same evidence guarantees as other topology processes.

A routed topology MUST NOT report readiness until every backend required by its routing policy and the public router endpoint are ready. Eval and Bench MUST attach only to the public workload endpoint.

The Inferlab-owned implementations MAY use static routing over configured ready backends. They MUST NOT be required to provide automatic restart, degraded-backend recovery, backend eviction, minimum-ready capacity, or production availability guarantees.

The vLLM integration MUST support `builtin` and `vllm-router` as distinct routing backends over both Mooncake and NIXL. `builtin` MUST resolve to the corresponding closed Inferlab implementation kind; `vllm-router` MUST resolve to `integration_native`. Their identities MUST NOT alias in resolution, commands, or evidence.

Routing evidence MUST identify ownership, selected backend, implementation identity and version, KV-transfer mechanism, effective routing policy, every rank-zero target, internal links and endpoints, and the public endpoint. Native routing evidence MUST additionally identify the integration version.

**Rationale:** Routing selection remains a P/D deployment behavior, but process ownership is a closed integration result. That lets Rust own built-in proxy lifecycle without asking Python to render it, lets native routers use framework-specific lowering and remote placement, and keeps SMG or another router distinct from Mooncake or NIXL transport.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0003:C-SGLANG-PREFILL-DECODE] SGLang Prefill Decode Routing (Normative) <a id="rfc-0003c-sglang-prefill-decode"></a>

The SGLang integration MUST support Mooncake and NIXL as selectable KV-transfer mechanisms for the prefill/decode topology defined by [RFC-0003:C-SERVE-TOPOLOGY](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-serve-topology).

Each of the `builtin` and `sglang-router` routing backends MUST support each of Mooncake and NIXL for the SGLang prefill/decode topology.

The SGLang integration MUST support independent positive replica counts for the prefill and decode roles.

For both KV-transfer mechanisms, the SGLang integration MUST declare one control-plane-allocated bootstrap endpoint for every prefill replica.

An SGLang prefill/decode topology MUST accept `builtin` and `sglang-router` as distinct routing-backend identities.

An SGLang prefill/decode topology MUST reject every other routing-backend identity during resolution before launching a process.

Selecting `builtin` MUST resolve to `inferlab_builtin` with implementation kind `sglang` and use the Inferlab-owned proxy and MUST NOT require a SGLang Model Gateway package in the serving workspace.

Selecting `sglang-router` MUST resolve to `integration_native` and render one SGLang Model Gateway process from the selected stack or image realization under [RFC-0003:C-EXPERIMENTAL-PROXY](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-experimental-proxy).

The two routing-backend identities MUST NOT alias one another in resolution, rendered commands, or record evidence.

Both routing backends MUST route only to the rank-zero serving entry point of each selected prefill and decode replica.

Initial server readiness with either routing backend MUST wait for every configured prefill replica, every configured decode replica, and the selected routing backend's public endpoint to become ready.

An integration-rendered SGLang Model Gateway MUST use its worker-aware readiness endpoint, which is unsuccessful until at least one prefill and one decode worker are healthy, rather than its process-liveness endpoint.

Before reporting an SGLang Model Gateway topology ready, Inferlab MUST verify through the Gateway worker registry that every configured rank-zero prefill and decode target is registered with its expected role, every prefill target has its expected bootstrap port, and every target is healthy.

For each completion request, the built-in proxy MUST select one configured prefill replica and one configured decode replica.

The built-in proxy MUST accept the scalar-prompt completion request shape consumed by Inferlab.

The built-in proxy MUST reject a prompt-array completion request before dispatching it to either role.

The built-in proxy MUST inject the selected prefill bootstrap host, bootstrap port, and one request-unique bootstrap room into the request delivered to both selected replicas.

The built-in proxy MUST initiate both role requests without waiting for a prefill generation response and MUST drain the prefill response.

When the request sets `stream` to false or omits it, the built-in proxy MUST return the complete decode response.

When the request sets `stream` to true, the built-in proxy MUST relay the decode response incrementally while preserving its status and content type.

A prefill or decode failure observed before public response headers are sent MUST produce an HTTP status outside 200 through 299.

A prefill or decode failure observed after response streaming begins MUST terminate the public stream as failed rather than complete it successfully.

Both routing backends MUST expose `/v1/completions` as the public completion endpoint.

Both routing backends MUST expose `POST /flush_cache` as the prefix-cache reset action through the public endpoint.

The public prefix-cache reset action MUST attempt the action on every configured prefill and decode replica.

The public prefix-cache reset action MUST return HTTP 200 only when every target succeeds and HTTP 206 when one or more targets fail.

Inferlab MUST record HTTP 206 from the public prefix-cache reset action as reset failure evidence.

Both routing backends MUST otherwise use the common routing, lifecycle, readiness, rollback, cleanup, and evidence requirements in [RFC-0003:C-EXPERIMENTAL-PROXY](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-experimental-proxy).

**Rationale:** The built-in proxy and SGLang Model Gateway serve complementary operator workflows: a dependency-light experiment baseline and the upstream routing implementation. Keeping their identities explicit preserves comparison evidence and prevents selecting one from silently changing the implementation. SGLang uses the prefill bootstrap rendezvous for both Mooncake and NIXL, so transport selection does not determine a vLLM-style side-channel shape. The built-in proxy intentionally supports only the completion shape Inferlab currently consumes, while Model Gateway remains available for upstream routing behavior. SGLang Model Gateway reports a partial cache flush with HTTP 206, so treating every 2xx response as reset success would create false measurement evidence. The generic role, replica, link, endpoint, and allocation shapes represent both routed topologies; proving that Model Gateway registered every configured target is a distinct readiness-boundary obligation.

*Since: v0.5.0*

### [RFC-0003:C-TENSORRT-LLM-PREFILL-DECODE] TensorRT-LLM Prefill Decode Routing (Normative) <a id="rfc-0003c-tensorrt-llm-prefill-decode"></a>

The TensorRT-LLM integration MUST support NIXL as the selectable KV-transfer mechanism for the prefill/decode topology defined by [RFC-0003:C-SERVE-TOPOLOGY](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-serve-topology).

The TensorRT-LLM integration MUST reject every other KV-transfer mechanism for this topology before launching a process.

The TensorRT-LLM integration MUST support independent positive replica counts for the prefill and decode roles.

A TensorRT-LLM prefill/decode topology MUST accept `builtin` and `trtllm-disaggregated` as distinct routing-backend identities.

A TensorRT-LLM prefill/decode topology MUST reject every other routing-backend identity during resolution before launching a process.

The two routing-backend identities MUST NOT alias one another in resolution, rendered commands, or record evidence.

Both routing backends MUST declare request-routing links from the routing role to the prefill and decode roles and one NIXL KV-transfer link from prefill to decode.

Both routing backends MUST NOT invent a control-plane bootstrap or side-channel endpoint when the selected TensorRT-LLM NIXL protocol exchanges its connection metadata in the context response.

Both routing backends MUST route only to the rank-zero serving entry point of each selected prefill and decode replica.

Each prefill and decode worker MUST receive an integration-rendered launch file under [RFC-0006:C-LAUNCH-FILES](../rfc/RFC-0006.md#rfc-0006c-launch-files).

The effective worker configuration MUST select the NIXL cache-transceiver backend and the Python cache-transceiver runtime.

The effective prefill-worker configuration MUST disable the overlap scheduler.

The effective KV-cache configuration of every prefill and decode worker MUST disable block reuse while the topology advertises no prefix-cache reset action.

An open TensorRT-LLM settings escape hatch MUST NOT override the selected cache-transceiver backend, cache-transceiver runtime, required prefill overlap setting, or required block-reuse setting.

Selecting `builtin` MUST resolve to `inferlab_builtin` with implementation kind `trtllm` and use an Inferlab-owned TensorRT-LLM proxy and MUST NOT require the native TensorRT-LLM disaggregated server.

For each completion request, the built-in proxy MUST select one configured prefill replica and one configured decode replica using independent static round-robin selection.

The built-in proxy MUST accept the scalar-string-prompt completion request shape consumed by Inferlab.

The built-in proxy MUST reject prompt arrays and a requested completion count other than one before dispatching to either role.

The built-in proxy MUST assign each dispatched completion a request-unique integer greater than or equal to `1 << 42` and MUST use that same `disagg_request_id` for its context and generation phases.

The built-in proxy MUST issue the selected prefill replica a non-streaming context-only request with context-first scheduling semantics.

The built-in proxy MUST reject a context response that has no first choice.

When the first context-response choice has finish reason `length` or `not_finished`, the built-in proxy MUST reject the response unless it contains a scalar-request prompt-token array, top-level usage, and a first-choice disaggregated-parameter object whose `ctx_request_id` is non-null, whose `disagg_request_id` equals the identifier assigned to the context request, and whose `first_gen_tokens` is present.

When the first context-response choice has a finish reason other than `length` or `not_finished`, the built-in proxy MUST NOT dispatch a decode request.

For a non-streaming public request that completes during the context phase, the built-in proxy MUST remove internal disaggregated parameters and return the complete context response.

For a streaming public request that completes during the context phase, the built-in proxy MUST return the terminal `data: [DONE]` server-sent event without dispatching a decode request.

When a generation handoff is required, the built-in proxy MUST replace the original prompt with the context response's prompt token identifiers.

The generation request MUST copy every member of the returned disaggregated-parameter object that the built-in proxy does not explicitly replace.

The generation request MUST replace only `request_type`, `schedule_style`, and `ctx_usage` in that object, setting them respectively to generation-only, context-first, and the context response's top-level usage.

When the public request is non-streaming, the built-in proxy MUST return the complete decode response.

When the public request is streaming, the built-in proxy MUST keep the context request non-streaming and relay the decode response incrementally while preserving its status and content type.

A context or decode failure observed before public response headers are sent MUST produce an HTTP status outside 200 through 299.

A decode failure observed after public response streaming begins MUST terminate the public stream as failed rather than complete it successfully.

Selecting `trtllm-disaggregated` MUST resolve to `integration_native` and render one native TensorRT-LLM disaggregated server from the selected stack or image realization under [RFC-0003:C-EXPERIMENTAL-PROXY](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-experimental-proxy).

The native disaggregated server MUST receive a static context-first round-robin configuration containing every configured rank-zero prefill and decode target through an integration-rendered launch file under [RFC-0006:C-LAUNCH-FILES](../rfc/RFC-0006.md#rfc-0006c-launch-files).

Initial server readiness with either routing backend MUST wait for every configured prefill replica, every configured decode replica, and the selected routing backend's public endpoint to become ready.

Both routing backends MUST expose `/v1/completions` as the public completion endpoint.

Neither routing backend MUST advertise a prefix-cache reset action for this workflow while the selected TensorRT-LLM serving interface exposes no action that can reset every configured prefill and decode worker.

Routing evidence for `builtin` MUST record the Inferlab-owned proxy identity and version.

Routing evidence for `trtllm-disaggregated` MUST record that backend identity and the TensorRT-LLM integration adapter version.

Both routing backends MUST otherwise use the common routing, lifecycle, readiness, rollback, cleanup, and evidence requirements in [RFC-0003:C-EXPERIMENTAL-PROXY](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-experimental-proxy).

**Rationale:** TensorRT-LLM context-first disaggregation is a two-stage request protocol: the context response carries prompt tokens, the first generated token, usage, and opaque connection metadata needed to construct the generation-only request. The built-in proxy is therefore a bounded TensorRT-LLM protocol adapter rather than the concurrent bootstrap proxy used by SGLang. Copying fields the proxy does not replace lets compatible upstream metadata evolve without a handwritten Inferlab projection silently discarding it. The native disaggregated server remains separately selectable so operators can compare an Inferlab-owned baseline with upstream orchestration while records retain the implementation difference. NIXL's TensorRT-LLM protocol carries its dynamic endpoint in response metadata, so requiring a vLLM-style side-channel port would record and allocate a topology that does not exist. Disabling block reuse provides repeatable measurement isolation while no composed reset action exists.

*Since: v0.6.0*

### [RFC-0003:C-TOKENSPEED-PREFILL-DECODE] TokenSpeed Prefill Decode Routing (Normative) <a id="rfc-0003c-tokenspeed-prefill-decode"></a>

The TokenSpeed integration MUST support the prefill/decode topology defined by [RFC-0003:C-SERVE-TOPOLOGY](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-serve-topology).

The topology's request-routing backend and KV-transfer mechanism MUST remain independently selected and independently recorded.

The TokenSpeed integration MUST accept Mooncake as the KV-transfer mechanism for this topology.

The TokenSpeed integration MUST reject every other KV-transfer mechanism during resolution before launching a process.

The TokenSpeed integration MUST support independent positive replica counts for the prefill and decode roles.

A TokenSpeed prefill/decode topology MUST accept `tokenspeed-smg` as its request-routing backend identity.

A TokenSpeed prefill/decode topology MUST reject every other request-routing backend identity during resolution before launching a process.

The topology MUST declare request-routing links from the router role to the prefill and decode roles, one Mooncake KV-transfer link from prefill to decode, and one control-plane-allocated bootstrap endpoint for every prefill replica.

Request routing, KV transfer, and bootstrap targeting MUST use only the rank-zero serving entry point of each selected prefill and decode replica.

Each rendered prefill worker MUST expose a TokenSpeed gRPC serving endpoint and MUST receive the prefill disaggregation role, the Mooncake transfer backend, and its allocated bootstrap endpoint.

Each rendered decode worker MUST expose a TokenSpeed gRPC serving endpoint and MUST receive the decode disaggregation role and the Mooncake transfer backend.

An open TokenSpeed settings escape hatch MUST NOT override the resolved disaggregation role, KV-transfer backend, model, served endpoint, process topology, parallelism, or allocated bootstrap endpoint.

Selecting `tokenspeed-smg` MUST resolve to `integration_native` and render one native SMG process from the selected stack or image realization under [RFC-0003:C-EXPERIMENTAL-PROXY](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-experimental-proxy).

The native SMG process MUST enable prefill/decode request routing and MUST receive a static target list containing every configured rank-zero prefill gRPC endpoint paired with its bootstrap endpoint and every configured rank-zero decode gRPC endpoint.

The native SMG process MUST use independent round-robin prefill and decode routing policies.

Initial server readiness MUST wait for every configured prefill replica, every configured decode replica, and the native SMG public endpoint to become ready.

Before reporting the topology ready, Inferlab MUST verify through the native SMG worker registry that every configured rank-zero gRPC target is registered with its expected role, every prefill target has its expected bootstrap port, and every target is healthy.

The public workload endpoint MUST expose `/v1/completions`.

The public endpoint MUST expose `POST /flush_cache` as its prefix-cache reset action.

The prefix-cache reset action MUST attempt to flush every configured prefill and decode worker and MUST report success only when every target succeeds.

Routing evidence MUST preserve the `tokenspeed-smg` identity, the independent round-robin policy, every rank-zero gRPC target and bootstrap endpoint, the public endpoint, and the TokenSpeed integration adapter version.

KV-transfer evidence and rendered worker commands MUST preserve Mooncake independently from the routing evidence.

The topology MUST otherwise use the common routing, lifecycle, readiness, rollback, cleanup, and evidence requirements in [RFC-0003:C-EXPERIMENTAL-PROXY](../rfc/RFC-0003.md#rfc-0003c-experimental-proxy).

**Rationale:** SMG owns request routing while Mooncake owns KV movement between the selected workers; treating them as separate axes keeps records truthful and allows either axis to evolve without inventing a composite backend identity. TokenSpeed's packaged gRPC servicer is the stable worker boundary consumed by native SMG, while Inferlab retains placement, lifecycle, readiness, and record authority. Registry-backed readiness prevents a live router process from hiding an omitted or unhealthy worker. SMG's composed cache-flush action provides one truthful public reset boundary across both roles.

*Since: v0.7.0*
